page: Microsoft Azure E64-16as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Easv7 family. It features an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 8 physical cores and 16 dedicated vCPUs, running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the top tier (top 10%). Similarly, single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text generation for small LLM models (135M) achieve top-tier performance. Multi-core operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, fall into the average category. Memory performance is robust, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, while large-block memory bandwidth read and write speeds remain average.

The high memory density relative to the vCPU count makes this server a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. It is well-suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, data buffering, and memory-intensive enterprise applications. The lack of local storage means workloads must rely on external Premium Storage, which should be factored into the overall architectural design.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64-16as_v7 is a memory-optimized server from the Easv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a high resource density of 32.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. While the server lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. In benchmark evaluations, the E64-16as_v7 delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression, and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong memory performance. Multi-core and database operations perform at average levels. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without proportional vCPU overhead.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16as_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring an AMD EPYC 9V45 CPU with 16 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16as_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 16 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high memory density and top-tier single-core performance for memory-bound enterprise workloads.

512.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance

Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-heavy workloads
